  "concepts": [
    { "id": "c-ai-safety-security", "name": "Safety and security of AI technology", "aliases": ["safe, secure, and trustworthy AI"], "description": "The overarching goal of ensuring AI systems are safe, secure, and trustworthy through robust evaluation, testing, and risk mitigation.", "grounding_span": "Ensuring the Safety and Security of AI Technology.", "location": "Sec. 4" },
    { "id": "c-red-teaming", "name": "AI red-teaming", "aliases": ["red-teaming", "red-team testing"], "description": "Structured adversarial testing to find flaws and vulnerabilities in an AI system.", "grounding_span": "a structured testing effort to find", "location": "Sec. 3(d)" },
    { "id": "c-dufm", "name": "Dual-use foundation model", "aliases": ["dual-use foundation models"], "description": "Broadly trained AI models with tens of billions of parameters capable of tasks posing serious security risks.", "grounding_span": "trained on broad data; generally uses self-supervision; contains at least tens", "location": "Sec. 3(k)" },
    { "id": "c-generative-ai", "name": "Generative AI", "aliases": ["generative artificial intelligence"], "description": "Class of AI models that emulate input data to generate derived synthetic content.", "grounding_span": "means the class of AI models that emulate", "location": "Sec. 3(p)" },
    { "id": "c-synthetic-content", "name": "Synthetic content", "aliases": ["AI-generated content"], "description": "Information significantly modified or generated by algorithms, including by AI.", "grounding_span": "videos, audio clips, and text, that has been significantly modified or generated", "location": "Sec. 3(ee)" },
    { "id": "c-watermarking", "name": "Watermarking", "aliases": ["content labeling"], "description": "Embedding hard-to-remove information into AI outputs to verify authenticity or provenance.", "grounding_span": "which is typically difficult to remove, into outputs created by AI", "location": "Sec. 3(gg)" },
    { "id": "c-provenance", "name": "Content authentication and provenance", "aliases": ["digital content authentication"], "description": "Establishing the authenticity and origin of digital content, synthetic and non-synthetic.", "grounding_span": "authenticating content and tracking its provenance;", "location": "Sec. 4.5(a)(i)" },
    { "id": "c-pets", "name": "Privacy-enhancing technologies", "aliases": ["PETs", "privacy-preserving technology"], "description": "Software/hardware means of mitigating privacy risks arising from data processing.", "grounding_span": "privacy-enhancing technologies (PETs) where appropriate,", "location": "Sec. 2(f)" },
    { "id": "c-diff-privacy", "name": "Differential privacy", "aliases": ["differential-privacy guarantee"], "description": "Protections allowing information about a group to be shared while provably limiting disclosure of individual data.", "grounding_span": "common risks to realizing differential privacy in", "location": "Sec. 9(b)" },
    { "id": "c-model-weights", "name": "Model weights", "aliases": ["model weight"], "description": "Numerical parameters within an AI model that determine its outputs.", "grounding_span": "the ownership and possession of the model weights of any dual", "location": "Sec. 4.2(a)(i)(B)" },
    { "id": "c-compute-cluster", "name": "Large-scale computing cluster", "aliases": ["compute threshold", "computing cluster"], "description": "Large co-located compute clusters subject to reporting requirements based on compute-capacity thresholds.", "grounding_span": "a potential large-scale computing cluster to report", "location": "Sec. 4.2(a)(ii)" },
    { "id": "c-cbrn", "name": "CBRN threats and AI", "aliases": ["chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear weapons"], "description": "Risk that AI is misused to assist in developing or using CBRN weapons.", "grounding_span": "Reducing Risks at the Intersection of AI and CBRN Threats.", "location": "Sec. 4.4" },
    { "id": "c-biosecurity", "name": "Biosecurity", "aliases": ["biosecurity risks"], "description": "Managing risks from AI applied to biology, especially generative models trained on biological data.", "grounding_span": "to reduce biosecurity risks, including recommendations on opportunities", "location": "Sec. 4.4(a)(ii)(C)" },
    { "id": "c-nucleic-screening", "name": "Synthetic nucleic acid procurement screening", "aliases": ["sequence synthesis screening"], "description": "Screening mechanisms for providers of synthetic nucleic acid sequences to manage biosecurity risk.", "grounding_span": "verifying the performance of sequence synthesis procurement screening,", "location": "Sec. 4.4(b)(i)(B)" },
    { "id": "c-critical-infrastructure", "name": "Critical infrastructure", "aliases": [], "description": "Systems and assets whose incapacity would harm security, defined per the USA PATRIOT Act.", "grounding_span": "1016(e) of the USA PATRIOT Act of 2001, 42 U.S.C. 5195c(e).", "location": "Sec. 3(i)" },
    { "id": "c-cyber-defense", "name": "AI for cyber defense", "aliases": ["AI cybersecurity"], "description": "Using AI capabilities such as LLMs to discover and remediate software vulnerabilities.", "grounding_span": "capitalize on AI’s potential to improve United States cyber defenses", "location": "Sec. 4.3(b)" },
    { "id": "c-algorithmic-discrimination", "name": "Algorithmic discrimination", "aliases": ["automated-system discrimination"], "description": "Discrimination in the use of automated systems, including AI.", "grounding_span": "including algorithmic discrimination", "location": "Sec. 7.1(a)(ii)" },
    { "id": "c-equity-civil-rights", "name": "Equity and civil rights", "aliases": [], "description": "Ensuring AI policy advances equity and complies with civil rights laws.", "grounding_span": "Advancing Equity and Civil Rights.", "location": "Sec. 7" },
    { "id": "c-ai-criminal-justice", "name": "AI in the criminal justice system", "aliases": [], "description": "Use of AI in sentencing, parole, bail, risk assessment, surveillance, and forensic analysis.", "grounding_span": "the use of AI in the criminal justice system,", "location": "Sec. 7.1(b)" },
    { "id": "c-predictive-policing", "name": "Crime forecasting and predictive policing", "aliases": [], "description": "Use of AI to predict crimes or crime hot spots by ingesting historical crime data.", "grounding_span": "crime forecasting and predictive policing, including the ingestion", "location": "Sec. 7.1(b)(i)(F)" },
    { "id": "c-ai-ip", "name": "AI and intellectual property", "aliases": ["AI inventorship", "AI and copyright"], "description": "Questions of inventorship, patent eligibility, and copyright at the intersection of AI and IP.", "grounding_span": "the treatment of copyrighted works in AI training.", "location": "Sec. 5.2(c)(iii)" },
    { "id": "c-ai-talent", "name": "AI talent in government", "aliases": ["AI workforce"], "description": "Recruiting, hiring, and retaining AI and AI-enabling talent across the Federal Government.", "grounding_span": "Increasing AI Talent in Government.", "location": "Sec. 10.2" },
    { "id": "c-worker-wellbeing", "name": "Worker well-being", "aliases": ["employee well-being"], "description": "Ensuring AI deployed in the workplace advances employees' well-being and job quality.", "grounding_span": "AI deployed in the workplace advances employees’", "location": "Sec. 6(b)" },
    { "id": "c-competition", "name": "Competition in AI markets", "aliases": [], "description": "Promoting fair competition and preventing dominant firms from disadvantaging AI competitors.", "grounding_span": "Promoting Competition.", "location": "Sec. 5.3" },
    { "id": "c-semiconductors", "name": "Semiconductors", "aliases": ["microelectronics"], "description": "Semiconductors as a critical input powering AI technologies and AI competition.", "grounding_span": "semiconductors power AI technologies", "location": "Sec. 5.3(b)" },
    { "id": "c-privacy", "name": "Privacy protection", "aliases": [], "description": "Mitigating privacy risks exacerbated by AI's collection, use, and inference on personal data.", "grounding_span": "Protecting Privacy.", "location": "Sec. 9" },
    { "id": "c-ai-risk-management", "name": "AI risk management", "aliases": [], "description": "Managing risks from AI as a distinct line of effort in standards and governance.", "grounding_span": "AI risk management", "location": "Sec. 11(b)(i)(D)" },
    { "id": "c-fed-use-ai", "name": "Federal Government use of AI", "aliases": [], "description": "Advancing and governing the use of AI across Federal agency programs and operations.", "grounding_span": "Advancing Federal Government Use of AI.", "location": "Sec. 10" },
    { "id": "c-caio", "name": "Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer", "aliases": ["Chief AI Officer", "CAIO"], "description": "Agency official with primary responsibility for coordinating AI use, innovation, and risk management.", "grounding_span": "designate their permanent Chief AI Officers", "location": "Sec. 10.1(a)" },
    { "id": "c-risk-mgmt-practices", "name": "Minimum risk-management practices", "aliases": [], "description": "Required minimum practices for government AI uses that impact people's rights or safety.", "grounding_span": "required minimum risk-management practices for Government uses", "location": "Sec. 10.1(b)(iv)" },
    { "id": "c-nairr", "name": "National AI Research Resource", "aliases": ["NAIRR"], "description": "Pilot integrating distributed compute, data, model, and training resources for AI research.", "grounding_span": "implementing the National AI Research Resource (NAIRR),", "location": "Sec. 5.2(a)(i)" },
    { "id": "c-intl-standards", "name": "International AI leadership and standards", "aliases": [], "description": "Strengthening U.S. leadership abroad and driving AI-related consensus standards.", "grounding_span": "Strengthening American Leadership Abroad.", "location": "Sec. 11" },
    { "id": "c-ai-healthcare", "name": "AI in healthcare and human services", "aliases": [], "description": "Safe, responsible deployment of AI and AI-enabled technologies in health and human services.", "grounding_span": "in the health and human services sector, using disaggregated data on", "location": "Sec. 8(b)(i)(C)" },
    { "id": "c-ai-education", "name": "AI in education", "aliases": [], "description": "Safe, responsible, and nondiscriminatory uses of AI in the education sector.", "grounding_span": "AI and the Future of Teaching and Learning", "location": "Sec. 8(d)" },
    { "id": "c-ai-transportation", "name": "AI in transportation", "aliases": [], "description": "Safe and responsible development and use of AI in the transportation sector.", "grounding_span": "the use of AI in transportation.", "location": "Sec. 8(c)(i)" },
    { "id": "c-ai-model", "name": "AI model", "aliases": [], "description": "A component of an information system implementing AI technology to produce outputs from inputs.", "grounding_span": "a component of an information system", "location": "Sec. 3(c)" },
    { "id": "c-ml", "name": "Machine learning", "aliases": [], "description": "A set of techniques used to train AI algorithms to improve task performance based on data.", "grounding_span": "a set of techniques that can be", "location": "Sec. 3(t)" }
  ],
  "definitions": [
    { "id": "def-agency", "term": "agency", "verbatim_text": "The term \"agency\" means each agency described in 44 U.S.C. 3502(1), except for the independent regulatory agencies described in 44 U.S.C. 3502(5).", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "each agency described in 44 U.S.C. 3502(1)", "location": "Sec. 3(a)" },
    { "id": "def-ai", "term": "artificial intelligence", "verbatim_text": "a machine-based system that can, for a given set of human-defined objectives, make predictions, recommendations, or decisions influencing real or virtual environments.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "a machine-based system that can, for a given set", "location": "Sec. 3(b)" },
    { "id": "def-ai-model", "term": "AI model", "verbatim_text": "The term \"AI model\" means a component of an information system that implements AI technology and uses computational, statistical, or machine-learning techniques to produce outputs from a given set of inputs.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "a component of an information system", "location": "Sec. 3(c)" },
    { "id": "def-ai-red-teaming", "term": "AI red-teaming", "verbatim_text": "The term \"AI red-teaming\" means a structured testing effort to find flaws and vulnerabilities in an AI system, often in a controlled environment and in collaboration with developers of AI.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "a structured testing effort to find", "location": "Sec. 3(d)" },
    { "id": "def-ai-system", "term": "AI system", "verbatim_text": "The term \"AI system\" means any data system, software, hardware, application, tool, or utility that operates in whole or in part using AI.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "operates in whole or in part using AI.", "location": "Sec. 3(e)" },
    { "id": "def-cai", "term": "commercially available information", "verbatim_text": "The term \"commercially available information\" means any information or data about an individual or group of individuals, including an individual's or group of individuals' device or location, that is made available or obtainable and sold, leased, or licensed to the general public or to governmental or non-governmental entities.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "sold, leased, or licensed to the general public", "location": "Sec. 3(f)" },
    { "id": "def-crime-forecasting", "term": "crime forecasting", "verbatim_text": "The term \"crime forecasting\" means the use of analytical techniques to attempt to predict future crimes or crime-related information.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "to attempt to predict future crimes or crime-related information.", "location": "Sec. 3(g)" },
    { "id": "def-cet", "term": "critical and emerging technologies", "verbatim_text": "The term \"critical and emerging technologies\" means those technologies listed in the February 2022 Critical and Emerging Technologies List Update issued by the National Science and Technology Council (NSTC), as amended by subsequent updates to the list issued by the NSTC.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "listed in the February 2022 Critical and Emerging Technologies List Update", "location": "Sec. 3(h)" },
    { "id": "def-critical-infrastructure", "term": "critical infrastructure", "verbatim_text": "The term \"critical infrastructure\" has the meaning set forth in section 1016(e) of the USA PATRIOT Act of 2001, 42 U.S.C. 5195c(e).", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "1016(e) of the USA PATRIOT Act of 2001, 42 U.S.C. 5195c(e).", "location": "Sec. 3(i)" },
    { "id": "def-diff-privacy", "term": "differential-privacy guarantee", "verbatim_text": "The term \"differential-privacy guarantee\" means protections that allow information about a group to be shared while provably limiting the improper access, use, or disclosure of personal information about particular entities.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "information about a group to be shared while provably limiting the improper", "location": "Sec. 3(j)" },
    { "id": "def-dufm", "term": "dual-use foundation model", "verbatim_text": "The term \"dual-use foundation model\" means an AI model that is trained on broad data; generally uses self-supervision; contains at least tens of billions of parameters; is applicable across a wide range of contexts; and that exhibits, or could be easily modified to exhibit, high levels of performance at tasks that pose a serious risk to security, national economic security, national public health or safety, or any combination of those matters.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "trained on broad data; generally uses self-supervision; contains at least tens", "location": "Sec. 3(k)" },
    { "id": "def-fed-le-agency", "term": "Federal law enforcement agency", "verbatim_text": "The term \"Federal law enforcement agency\" has the meaning set forth in section 21(a) of Executive Order 14074 of May 25, 2022.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "in section 21(a) of Executive Order 14074 of May 25, 2022", "location": "Sec. 3(l)" },
    { "id": "def-flop", "term": "floating-point operation", "verbatim_text": "The term \"floating-point operation\" means any mathematical operation or assignment involving floating-point numbers, which are a subset of the real numbers typically represented on computers by an integer of fixed precision scaled by an integer exponent of a fixed base.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "or assignment involving floating-point numbers, which are a subset of the", "location": "Sec. 3(m)" },
    { "id": "def-foreign-person", "term": "foreign person", "verbatim_text": "The term \"foreign person\" has the meaning set forth in section 5(c) of Executive Order 13984 of January 19, 2021.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "has the meaning set forth in section 5(c)", "location": "Sec. 3(n)" },
    { "id": "def-foreign-reseller", "term": "foreign reseller", "verbatim_text": "The terms \"foreign reseller\" and \"foreign reseller of United States Infrastructure as a Service Products\" mean a foreign person who has established an Infrastructure as a Service Account to provide Infrastructure as a Service Products subsequently, in whole or in part, to a third party.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "established an Infrastructure as a Service Account to provide Infrastructure as", "location": "Sec. 3(o)" },
    { "id": "def-generative-ai", "term": "generative AI", "verbatim_text": "The term \"generative AI\" means the class of AI models that emulate the structure and characteristics of input data in order to generate derived synthetic content. This can include images, videos, audio, text, and other digital content.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "means the class of AI models that emulate", "location": "Sec. 3(p)" },
    { "id": "def-iaas", "term": "Infrastructure as a Service Product", "verbatim_text": "The terms \"Infrastructure as a Service Product,\" \"United States Infrastructure as a Service Product,\" \"United States Infrastructure as a Service Provider,\" and \"Infrastructure as a Service Account\" each have the respective meanings given to those terms in section 5 of Executive Order 13984.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "meanings given to those terms in section 5 of Executive Order 13984.", "location": "Sec. 3(q)" },
    { "id": "def-integer-op", "term": "integer operation", "verbatim_text": "The term \"integer operation\" means any mathematical operation or assignment involving only integers, or whole numbers expressed without a decimal point.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "assignment involving only integers, or whole numbers expressed without", "location": "Sec. 3(r)" },
    { "id": "def-intel-community", "term": "Intelligence Community", "verbatim_text": "The term \"Intelligence Community\" has the meaning given to that term in section 3.5(h) of Executive Order 12333 of December 4, 1981, as amended.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "in section 3.5(h) of Executive Order 12333 of December 4, 1981", "location": "Sec. 3(s)" },
    { "id": "def-ml", "term": "machine learning", "verbatim_text": "The term \"machine learning\" means a set of techniques that can be used to train AI algorithms to improve performance at a task based on data.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "used to train AI algorithms to improve performance at a task based on", "location": "Sec. 3(t)" },
    { "id": "def-model-weight", "term": "model weight", "verbatim_text": "The term \"model weight\" means a numerical parameter within an AI model that helps determine the model's outputs in response to inputs.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "AI model that helps determine the model’s outputs in response to inputs.", "location": "Sec. 3(u)" },
    { "id": "def-nss", "term": "national security system", "verbatim_text": "The term \"national security system\" has the meaning set forth in 44 U.S.C. 3552(b)(6).", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "44 U.S.C. 3552(b)(6).", "location": "Sec. 3(v)" },
    { "id": "def-omics", "term": "omics", "verbatim_text": "The term \"omics\" means biomolecules, including nucleic acids, proteins, and metabolites, that make up a cell or cellular system.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "that make up a cell or cellular system.", "location": "Sec. 3(w)" },
    { "id": "def-open-ran", "term": "Open RAN", "verbatim_text": "The term \"Open RAN\" means the Open Radio Access Network approach to telecommunications-network standardization adopted by the O–RAN Alliance, Third Generation Partnership Project, or any similar set of published open standards for multi-vendor network equipment interoperability.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "the Open Radio Access Network approach", "location": "Sec. 3(x)" },
    { "id": "def-pii", "term": "personally identifiable information", "verbatim_text": "The term \"personally identifiable information\" has the meaning set forth in Office of Management and Budget (OMB) Circular No. A–130.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "forth in Office of Management and Budget (OMB) Circular No. A–130.", "location": "Sec. 3(y)" },
    { "id": "def-pet", "term": "privacy-enhancing technology", "verbatim_text": "The term \"privacy-enhancing technology\" means any software or hardware solution, technical process, technique, or other technological means of mitigating privacy risks arising from data processing. This is also sometimes referred to as \"privacy-preserving technology.\"", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "This is also sometimes referred to as ‘‘privacy-preserving technology.’’", "location": "Sec. 3(z)" },
    { "id": "def-pia", "term": "privacy impact assessment", "verbatim_text": "The term \"privacy impact assessment\" has the meaning set forth in OMB Circular No. A–130.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "in OMB Circular No. A–130.", "location": "Sec. 3(aa)" },
    { "id": "def-srma", "term": "Sector Risk Management Agency", "verbatim_text": "The term \"Sector Risk Management Agency\" has the meaning set forth in 6 U.S.C. 650(23).", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "forth in 6 U.S.C. 650(23).", "location": "Sec. 3(bb)" },
    { "id": "def-self-healing", "term": "self-healing network", "verbatim_text": "The term \"self-healing network\" means a telecommunications network that automatically diagnoses and addresses network issues to permit self-restoration.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "means a telecommunications network", "location": "Sec. 3(cc)" },
    { "id": "def-synbio", "term": "synthetic biology", "verbatim_text": "The term \"synthetic biology\" means a field of science that involves redesigning organisms, or the biomolecules of organisms, at the genetic level to give them new characteristics.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "redesigning organisms, or the biomolecules of organisms, at the genetic", "location": "Sec. 3(dd)" },
    { "id": "def-synthetic-content", "term": "synthetic content", "verbatim_text": "The term \"synthetic content\" means information, such as images, videos, audio clips, and text, that has been significantly modified or generated by algorithms, including by AI.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "videos, audio clips, and text, that has been significantly modified or generated", "location": "Sec. 3(ee)" },
    { "id": "def-testbed", "term": "testbed", "verbatim_text": "The term \"testbed\" means a facility or mechanism equipped for conducting rigorous, transparent, and replicable testing of tools and technologies, including AI and PETs, to help evaluate the functionality, usability, and performance of those tools or technologies.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "including AI and PETs, to help evaluate the functionality, usability, and", "location": "Sec. 3(ff)" },
    { "id": "def-watermarking", "term": "watermarking", "verbatim_text": "The term \"watermarking\" means the act of embedding information, which is typically difficult to remove, into outputs created by AI for the purposes of verifying the authenticity of the output or the identity or characteristics of its provenance, modifications, or conveyance.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2023-10-30", "grounding_span": "which is typically difficult to remove, into outputs created by AI", "location": "Sec. 3(gg)" }
  ],
  "claims": [
    { "id": "claim-inequities", "claim_text": "AI systems deployed irresponsibly have reproduced and intensified existing inequities, caused new types of harmful discrimination, and exacerbated online and physical harms.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Intelligence systems deployed irresponsibly have reproduced and intensified", "location": "Sec. 2(d)" },
    { "id": "claim-semiconductors", "claim_text": "Semiconductors power AI technologies and their availability is critical to AI competition.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "semiconductors power AI technologies", "location": "Sec. 5.3(b)" },
    { "id": "claim-privacy-inference", "claim_text": "Artificial Intelligence is making it easier to extract, re-identify, link, infer, and act on sensitive information about people's identities, locations, habits, and desires.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Intelligence is making it easier to extract, re-identify,", "location": "Sec. 2(f)" },
    { "id": "claim-pace", "claim_text": "The rapid speed at which AI capabilities are advancing compels the United States to lead.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "at which AI capabilities are advancing compels the United States to lead", "location": "Sec. 1" },
    { "id": "claim-open-weights", "claim_text": "When model weights for a dual-use foundation model are widely available there can be substantial benefits to innovation, but also substantial security risks.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "there can be substantial benefits to innovation, but also substantial security", "location": "Sec. 4.6" },
    { "id": "claim-ai-reflects", "claim_text": "AI reflects the principles of the people who build it, the people who use it, and the data upon which it is built.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "AI reflects the principles of the people who build it, the people", "location": "Sec. 1" }
  ],
